differences.title: 1. SCALING
differences.leftDesc: Scales up/down automatically based on requests. No infrastructure to manage.
differences.rightDesc: Scales containers in groups. Requires configuration for auto-scaling policies.

differences.title: 2. COST MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Only pay when your code runs. Idle time costs nothing.
differences.rightDesc: Pay for running instances/resources, even if idle.

differences.title: 3. VENDOR LOCK-IN
differences.leftDesc: Tied to specific cloud provider's FaaS implementation.

differences.title: 4. COLD STARTS
differences.leftDesc: Initial delay when a function hasn't been invoked recently.
differences.rightDesc: Containers are generally kept warm or start faster.

differences.title: 5. EXECUTION DURATION
differences.leftDesc: Functions often have maximum execution time limits.
differences.rightDesc: Containers can run indefinitely if needed.

differences.title: 6. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.rightDesc: Can maintain application state within the container.

differences.title: 7. DEVELOPMENT COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Focus on single-task functions, easier to develop and deploy.
differences.rightDesc: Requires managing container images, orchestration, and dependencies.
